data.change_summary: Tightened milestone task-run lineage so explicit new runs no longer backfill a sole legacy runless start row, which preserves retry audit history after upgrades. I also rewrote task-run attempt collapsing to pair legacy start/completion rows sequentially instead of assuming `started_at` is unique, so same-timestamp runless retries stay visible in `find_runs_for_bead`, and added regressions for both behaviors.
outstanding_risks: Milestone mutations still span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction, so a crash between file writes can still leave snapshot or journal state temporarily behind `task-runs.ndjson` until the same operation is replayed or state is reconciled from lineage.

Legacy attempts that still omit `run_id` remain inherently ambiguous for completion updates when multiple same-project attempts are open at once; this change preserves those attempts in audit queries instead of collapsing them, but deterministic finalization still depends on richer caller metadata.

steps.description: Inspected the current `TaskRunEntry` matching, collapse, and filesystem start paths against the two pending amendments to isolate where legacy runless rows were being reused or over-collapsed.

Updated `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs` so named runs only match exact `run_id`s, legacy no-`run_id` comparisons no longer collapse terminal-terminal rows, and attempt collapsing now groups legacy rows sequentially instead of depending on timestamp uniqueness.

Adjusted `src/adapters/fs.rs` start handling so an explicit retry can append a new attempt alongside a stale legacy open row instead of rewriting it, while still rejecting genuinely ambiguous multi-open cases.

Added regression coverage in `model.rs` and `service.rs` for same-timestamp legacy retries staying visible and for upgraded explicit retries appending a new attempt after a legacy open row, then revalidated the milestone_record path.
